summary: The checkout has no commits ahead of origin/main, and recent history contains no #3317 or distinctive-title merge. The cleanup is largely absent: pyproject.toml still directly declares llama-index, orjson, watchfiles, aiohttp, nltk, and exact click 8.4.2, while poetry.lock still contains the umbrella-only packages. The existing explicit LlamaIndex integration declarations satisfy one acceptance criterion, so the conservative verdict is PARTIALLY_IMPLEMENTED rather than NOT_IMPLEMENTED. No test suites were run per this read-only assessment step.

requirements.description: llama-index is no longer a direct dependency.
llama-index-core is explicitly declared at a compatible version.
Every active LlamaIndex integration import is backed by an explicit dependency.
Unused umbrella transitive packages are removed from the lockfile, or their remaining parent dependency is documented.
orjson is removed as a direct dependency after confirming no runtime use.
watchfiles is development-only and absent from the production main export unless required transitively.
aiohttp is no longer an unconditional direct production dependency; any test-only requirement is declared explicitly for tests.
The exact direct Click pin is removed, or a verified compatibility reason for retaining it is documented.
nltk is no longer a direct dependency; any transitive presence is accurately reported.
The moonmind.vision package is either removed after a complete reference audit or retained with the blocking runtime caller documented.
poetry.lock is regenerated and poetry check --lock passes.
Production and test dependency exports install successfully in clean environments; this requires clean-environment installation verification excluded by the instruction not to run long suites.
FastAPI startup/import succeeds with production dependencies only; this requires a clean main-only environment not available within this read-only assessment.
Required backend CI and targeted dependency-boundary tests pass; long verification suites were explicitly excluded from this step.
No API route, request/response schema, provider default, embedding behavior, ingestion behavior, or RAG behavior changes.
The PR description includes before/after dependency evidence and notes any unexpected retained transitive packages.
